social_campaign_topics.title: Copilot vs Codex
social_campaign_topics.hook: Copilot writes faster. Codex cleans up better.
social_campaign_topics.angle: Copilot is useful for lightweight autocomplete, but Codex is where I usually go when the repo needs deeper repair.

social_campaign_topics.title: Cursor vs Codex
social_campaign_topics.hook: Cursor is fast inside a clean repo. Codex is better when the project is already broken.
social_campaign_topics.angle: Cursor helps me iterate in context, while Codex is stronger when the fix requires architecture-level reasoning.

social_campaign_topics.title: Windsurf + Codex workflow
social_campaign_topics.hook: Windsurf is fast for scaffolding, but Codex is where I usually repair the mess.
social_campaign_topics.angle: The workflow that works best for me is rough structure first, then logic repair, build fixes, and final cleanup.

social_campaign_topics.title: When Codex fixes what Cursor breaks
social_campaign_topics.topic: When Codex fixes what Cursor breaks
social_campaign_topics.hook: The second failed fix is the real AI coding benchmark.
social_campaign_topics.angle: Cursor is useful for fast repo edits, but repeated small fixes can loop. That is when I move the problem to Codex.

social_campaign_topics.title: Why I stopped using only one AI coding tool
social_campaign_topics.topic: Why I stopped using only one AI coding tool
social_campaign_topics.hook: I stopped asking which AI coding tool is best. I started asking which one fits each stage.
social_campaign_topics.angle: One tool rarely wins every stage: bootstrapping, debugging, refactoring, deployment, and final review behave differently.

social_campaign_topics.title: My actual AI coding workflow in 2026
social_campaign_topics.topic: My actual AI coding workflow in 2026
social_campaign_topics.hook: My current workflow: Windsurf for rough structure, Codex for repair, Cursor for iteration, Copilot for light autocomplete.
social_campaign_topics.angle: The stack only works when each tool has a job. If every tool is asked to do everything, cleanup gets expensive.

social_campaign_topics.title: Which AI survives the second failed fix?
social_campaign_topics.topic: Which AI survives the second failed fix?
social_campaign_topics.hook: Most AI coding demos collapse when the repo gets messy.
social_campaign_topics.angle: The real benchmark is not the first generated answer. It is what happens after two fixes fail and the build is still red.

content_angles.title: Beginner guide
content_angles.hook: The mistake beginners make is asking one AI coding tool to do every stage.
content_angles.summary: Use the topic to explain the first practical decision: which tool handles setup, which one handles repair, and which one should stay in autocomplete mode.

content_angles.title: Advanced debugging
content_angles.hook: The second failed fix is where AI coding tools stop looking equal.
content_angles.summary: Focus on repo context, build errors, duplicated logic, and how the tool behaves after the first confident answer fails.

content_angles.title: Workflow tip
content_angles.hook: The best AI coding workflow is usually a handoff, not a single-tool bet.
content_angles.summary: Show a practical sequence: scaffold, repair, iterate, review, then deploy.

content_angles.title: Failure case
content_angles.hook: Most AI coding demos hide the cleanup step.
content_angles.summary: Use a realistic failure: repeated patches, broken build, weak context, or architecture drift.

content_angles.title: Hot take
content_angles.hook: Autocomplete speed is not the same as engineering leverage.
content_angles.summary: Challenge the usual tool ranking and explain why the winner changes by project stage.

content_angles.title: Pricing reality
content_angles.hook: The expensive part of AI coding tools is not always the monthly plan.
content_angles.summary: Talk about cleanup time, review cost, team adoption, and when a cheaper tool becomes expensive.

content_angles.title: Architecture scaling
content_angles.hook: AI coding tools are easy to like until the architecture starts drifting.
content_angles.summary: Frame the topic around module boundaries, repeated logic, and what happens when the repo grows.

content_angles.title: Repo context
content_angles.hook: Repo context beats demo speed once the project stops being clean.
content_angles.summary: Compare how tools read existing files, keep context, and avoid repeating failed fixes.

content_angles.title: Startup team
content_angles.hook: For small teams, the best AI coding tool is the one that does not create review debt.
content_angles.summary: Focus on adoption, code review, handoff, and risk for lean teams.

content_angles.title: Solo builder
content_angles.hook: Solo builders do not need the flashiest AI tool. They need the least cleanup drag.
content_angles.summary: Discuss speed, mental load, debugging fatigue, and shipping alone.

content_angles.title: Productivity myth
content_angles.hook: AI coding productivity is not measured by how much code appears on screen.
content_angles.summary: Separate generation speed from shipped, reviewed, working code.

content_angles.title: Second failed fix
content_angles.hook: The second failed fix is my favorite AI coding benchmark.
content_angles.summary: Make the repeated-fix moment the main test for reasoning, context, and tool limits.

content_angles.title: AI limitations
content_angles.hook: The more I use AI coding tools, the more I respect their limits.
content_angles.summary: Discuss what should stay human-reviewed: architecture, security, policy, deployment, and final judgment.

content_angles.title: Real-world tradeoff
content_angles.hook: Every AI coding tool tradeoff shows up after the first clean demo.
content_angles.summary: Compare speed, context, stability, adoption, and debugging cost without making any tool perfect.

content_angles.title: Migration story
content_angles.hook: Switching AI coding tools only helps if the workflow changes too.
content_angles.summary: Explain what changes when moving from one tool to a staged workflow across Cursor, Windsurf, Codex, and Copilot.
